query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Impediment', 'A hindrance or obstruction', 'The essay highlights the impediment of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impedius', 'Hinder or obstruct', 'The essay highlights the impedius of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impel', 'To urge a person, to press on, to incite to action or motion', 'The author hoped the new policy would impel tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impend', 'To be about to happen or occur', 'Critics argued that the plan might impend the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impenetrable', 'Impossible to pass through or understand', '“The dense forest was nearly impenetrable.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impenitent', 'Not feeling regret over one''s sins', 'The reviewer described the tone as impenitent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imperative', 'Of vital importance; crucial', '“It is imperative that we address climate change immediately.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imperceptible', 'Impossible to perceive', '“The difference in weight was imperceptible to the touch.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imperfectible', 'Incapable of being made perfect', 'Scholars noted a striking imperfectible between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imperial', 'Related to an empire, emperor, or empress', 'The author hoped the new policy would imperial tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imperil', 'To put into peril, to place in danger or cause a hazard', 'The author hoped the new policy would imperil tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imperious', 'Arrogantly commanding', 'What seemed imperious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impermissible', 'Not permissible, not to be permitted or allowed', 'Over time, the community learned to impermissible its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impersonal', 'Not personal, not having personality', 'What seemed impersonal at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impersonate', 'To assume the character or appearance of', 'Critics argued that the plan might impersonate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impersuadable', 'Not to be persuaded, obstinate, unyielding', 'Critics argued that the plan might impersuadable the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impertinent', 'Insolent, ill-mannered', 'The passage presents a impertinent portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imperturbable', 'Incapable of being upset or agitated, not easily excited', 'The reviewer described the tone as imperturbable, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impervious', 'Not permitting penetration or passage, impenetrable', 'What seemed impervious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impetuosity', 'The quality of making rash or arbitrary decisions', 'The essay highlights the impetuosity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impetuous', 'Acting quickly without thought or care', '“His impetuous decision to quit his job surprised everyone.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impetus', 'Something that impels, a stimulating factor', 'Her impetus became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impiety', 'The state of being impious', 'The essay highlights the impiety of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impinge', 'Encroach on', 'Her impinge became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impious', 'Not pious or religious, lacking reverence for god, etc', 'The reviewer described the tone as impious, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Implacable', 'Unable to be appeased or placated', 'The author hoped the new policy would implacable tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Implausible', 'Not plausible, unlikely, dubious', 'The passage presents a implausible portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Implement', 'To bring about, to put into practice', 'Over time, the community learned to implement its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impliable', 'Not pliable, inflexible, unyielding', 'The passage presents a impliable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Implicate', 'To show to be involved, usually in an incriminating manner', 'The author hoped the new policy would implicate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Implication', 'A conclusion that can be drawn from something, though not explicitly stated', '“The implication of his statement was clear—he wanted a promotion.”', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Implicit', 'Implied or understood without being directly expressed; inherent', 'Scholars noted a striking implicit between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imply', 'To indicate or suggest without being explicitly stated', 'She spoke imply, leaving little doubt about her position.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impolitic', 'Not in accordance with good policy, unwise, inexpedient', 'The passage presents a impolitic portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Importation', 'The act or an instance of importing', 'The essay highlights the importation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Importunate', 'To importune, or to obtain by importunity', 'Over time, the community learned to importunate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Importune', 'To harass with persistent requests', 'The author hoped the new policy would importune tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impos', 'Having a striking appearance or commanding presence', 'What seemed impos at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imposture', 'The action or practice of imposing fraudulently upon others', 'The essay highlights the imposture of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impotent', 'Lacking physical strength or vigor, weak', 'What seemed impotent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impoverish', 'Make poor', 'Her impoverish became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impracticable', 'Not practicable, impossible or difficult in practice', 'The passage presents a impracticable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imprecation', 'The act of imprecating, or invoking evil upon someone', 'Her imprecation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imprecise', 'Not exact or accurate', '“His instructions were too imprecise to be useful.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impregnate', 'To cause to become pregnant', 'The author hoped the new policy would impregnate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impromptu', 'Improvised, without prior preparation, unplanned', 'The essay highlights the impromptu of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Improper', 'Not proper, not strictly belonging, applicable, etc', 'Scholars noted a striking improper between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Improvident', 'Failing to provide for the future, reckless', 'The author hoped the new policy would improvident tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Improvise', 'To compose and perform without previous preparation', 'The essay highlights the improvise of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imprudent', 'Not prudent, wanting in prudence or discretion, indiscreet', 'What seemed imprudent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
